title: Chrysanthemum delavayi
intro: Chrysanthemum delavayi, also known as the Yunnan chrysanthemum, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The Yunnan chrysanthemum has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate fever, relieve respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the Yunnan chrysanthemum is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum delavayi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Chrysanthemum, but it is often included in the genus Leucanthemum. This plant is native to China and is known for its showy white or yellow flowers. The classification of Chrysanthemum delavayi may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered a member of the daisy family. This classification allows us to understand the plant's relationships with other members of the Asteraceae family.
